neighborhoods.name: Ciudad Amurallada (Walled City)
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO World Heritage walled center with colorful colonial architecture, plazas, and rooftop bars

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial mansions and boutique apartment conversions

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Cartagena. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Manga
neighborhoods.description: Residential island neighborhood connected by bridge with tree-lined streets and Republican-era mansions
neighborhoods.character: Quiet island residential, historic character, family-friendly

housing.predominantType: Restored mansions, modern condos, and townhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Cartagena's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Ternera / Turbaco
neighborhoods.description: Growing outer area with new residential developments and lower costs of living

housing.predominantType: New construction apartments and affordable housing developments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Bocagrande
neighborhoods.description: Modern beachfront peninsula with high-rise hotels, condos, and a cosmopolitan atmosphere
neighborhoods.character: Beach resort, high-rise, tourist and expat hub

housing.predominantType: High-rise condominiums and luxury apartment towers

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
